[PDF_PAGE 121 | score=9] Greek Heroes W Herades (de migo d) The son of Zeus and the mortal woman Al­ cmeoc, Herades was strength personified. While still an infant, he strangled Iwo huge snakes with his bare hands. Of course, Zeus ' wife. Hera, was jealous of the affair that begot Heracles, and she is always conspiring to bring him harm. Herades is a fearless adventurer whose many escapades are the sluff of leg­ end. A robust , cheerful man , he has an appetite for food and women that almost equals thai of his divine father. Although he visits Olympus from time to time, and has been known to aid the gods in their struggles . Heracles spends most of his lime in the world of men. He isa stou l man with a long beard, u

[PDF_PAGE 93 | score=6] Ra (greater god) Ra is the father of the Egyptian pantheon . He gave birth to Shu, who became the air. and Te£nut, who be<:ame moisture. He is the grandfather of Geb (the earth) and Nut (the sky), and the great-grandfathe r of Osiris. Isis, Set, and Nephythys. Together, these gods form the Great Ennead of Heliopolis . the city of the sun . He is said to have created mortal man from his tears . Every day, Ra rises above Manu (the hill of sunrises) aboard his great boat Manic! and looks down upon the world with his blazing eye (the sun).
